title: AV-21 Landspeeder

The '''AV-21 Landspeeder''' was a rare [[Mobquet Swoops and Speeders/Legends|Mobquet]] high-performance [[Landspeeder/Legends|landspeeder]] able to carry two to four people. The [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] originally suspected the [[Speeder/Legends|speeder]] to be a prototype for a fast attack vehicle developed by the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]]. However, it was in actuality a limited-production landspeeder highly sought after by speed freaks, [[Racer/Legends|racing]] aficionados, and various stripes of wealthy elite.

==Characteristics==
[[File:AV-21_landspeeder_SoT.png|thumb|left|250px|An AV-21 Landspeeder]]
The AV-21 was a limited-production [[Landspeeder/Legends|landspeeder]] built for raw straight-line speed rather than delicate handling. Long, low, and lean, the AV-21 featured a striking wasp-waisted profile that made the [[Speeder/Legends|speeder]] look fast even while standing still. From the front, the speeder looked like a small [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]], with a forward [[Hull/Legends|hull]] section that resembled a smaller, narrower [[BTL Y-wing starfighter/Legends|Y-wing]] [[Cockpit/Legends|cockpit]]. A small, well-appointed, two-seat cockpit was located in the middle of the vehicle.<ref name="Target" /> However, other configurations of the speeder allowed for a capacity of up to three passengers in addition to the [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]].<ref name="Advocate" /> The vehicle's aft section was dominated by razor-thin control surfaces and two massive [[Ion engine/Legends|ion thrusters]] in streamlined outboard nacelles. Every panel was precision-made and [[Limb/Legends|hand-fitted]] to make as smooth and aerodynamically efficient a surface as possible in an effort to squeeze every ounce of speed out of the powerful [[Engine/Legends|engines]]. The AV-21's maximum altitude was 3 [[Meter/Legends|meters]],<ref name="Target" /> and its top speed and acceleration rate was comparable to that offered by a [[Zephyr-G swoop/Legends|Zephyr-G swoop]] or [[BARC speeder/Legends|BARC speeder]].<ref name="ConsumerReport1">{{SonyCite|url=en_US/players/content.vm?id=66857&resource=features|text=''Corellia Times'' Consumer Reports: Vehicles}}</ref>

==History==
[[File:AV21landspeeder-TSS.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Rebels in an AV-21 under fire]]
Each AV-21 was almost completely hand-built in a special [[Mobquet Swoops and Speeders/Legends|Mobquet]] facility as a limited-production landspeeder. This contributed to its rarity,<ref name="Target" /> and the ''[[Corellia Times]]'' reported on its reputation as being a speeder of unknown origin. The [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] originally assumed the rare and sleek vehicle to be a prototype for a [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] fast attack vehicle.<ref name="ConsumerReport1" /> While the Rebel Alliance did make use of the speeder,<ref name="AV21TCG" /> the AV-21 was in actuality a [[Racer/Legends|racing]] vehicle highly sought after by speed freaks, racing aficionados, and various stripes of wealthy elite as both a status symbol and as a stunning example of the landspeeder builder's art. While the speeder retailed for a price of 6,500 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]], the waiting list to purchase a new one was over a [[Standard year/Legends|standard year long]]. If one was found, it would often sell for two to four times the standard price.<ref name="Target" /> By around [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]],<ref name="SWG_timeline">{{SWG_timeline}}</ref> schematics for the AV-21 fell into the possession of various factions, including the Rebel Alliance, [[Jabba's criminal empire/Legends|Jabba Desilijic Tiure's criminal empire]], and the Empire,<ref name="SWG:CC">{{SWGcite|exp=AED|type=heroic|Corellian Corvette}}</ref> the latter of which had their [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] use larger [[Transport/Legends|transport]] versions of the speeder in at least some capacity.<ref name="Advocate" />

{{Gamemechanics|Star Wars Galaxies}}
These schematics were at [[Time/Legends|times]] awarded to [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] in recognition of their service. The Empire, for instance, bestowed AV-21 schematics to their recipients of various medals, as did the Rebel Alliance. Underlings of [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba Desilijic Tiure]] likewise received these schematics as a gift for demonstrating courage, valor, and heroism in service of the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]].<ref name="SWG:CC" />
{{Endgame}}
So long as they had access to these schematics and the requisite technical knowledge, independent [[Artisan/Legends|artisans]] and [[Engineer/Legends|engineers]] were capable of producing AV-21 Landspeeders themselves. However, this still necessitated a supply of the various parts and metal materials, including a specialized AV-21 [[Power plant/Legends|power plant]].<ref name="Schematic">{{SWGcite|exp=AED|type=item|Landspeeder Schematic (AV-21)}}</ref><ref name="SchematicNGE">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=item|Landspeeder Schematic (AV-21)}}</ref> These power plants were sometimes ferried aboard [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 Corellian corvettes]], where they could be found stowed within the vessel's [[Armory/Legends|armory]] cabinets.<ref name="ConsumerReport1" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Advocate.png|thumb|left|200px|Imperial stormtroopers piloting an AV-21 with a larger seating capacity]]
The AV-21 Landspeeder was a [[W:c:swg:Vehicle|vehicle]] in the [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] [[Star Wars Galaxies|''Star Wars Galaxies: An Empire Divided'']], a [[Roleplaying game|massively multiplayer online role-playing game]] developed by [[Sony]] Online Entertainment and published by [[LucasArts]],<ref name="AED">{{GalaxiesAED}}</ref> prior to its closure on [[December 15]], [[2011]].<ref name="Shutdown">{{SonyCite|url=playerinfo|text=IMPORTANT INFORMATION ABOUT ''STAR WARS'' GALAXIES™}}</ref> The vehicle was introduced on [[April 13]], [[2004]] as part of the [[W:c:swg:Publish 7|Publish 7 Droid Invasion]] update, which introduced a new series of [[W:c:swg:Instance|instanced]] [[W:c:swg:Instanced dungeon|dungeon]] [[W:c:swg:Quest|quests]] featuring the [[W:c:swg:Guide to the Corellian Corvette|Corellian corvette]].<ref name="Publish7">{{SonyCite|url=recent_updates.jsp?o=61441|text=Publish 7: Droid Invasion}}</ref> Nine quests were added in total, including three destroy missions, three [[Assassin/Legends|assassination]] missions, and three rescue missions for [[W:c:swg:Galactic Empire|Imperial]], [[W:c:swg:Rebel Alliance|Rebel]], and neutral players. Players who completed any one of these nine quests would receive an [[W:c:swg:AV-21 Landspeeder (Schematic)|AV-21 Landspeeder schematic]] as a reward.<ref name="SWG:CC" /> [[W:c:swg:Artisan|Artisans]] could then construct the speeder with [[W:c:swg:Ferrous metal|ferrous]] and [[W:c:swg:Non-ferrous metal|non-ferous metals]], in addition to another key [[W:c:swg:Component|component]], an [[W:c:swg:Power Plant AV-21|AV-21 power plant]].<ref name="Schematic" /> These power plants could primarily be found by looting armory containers onboard the Corellian corvettes.<ref name="ConsumerReport1" />

[[File:Av21landspeeder.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The AV-21 first appeared in ''Star Wars Galaxies''.]]
With the release of [[W:c:swg:Publish 25 Notes for November 15 2005|Publish 25's]] [[W:c:swg:New Game Enhancements|New Game Enhancements]] on [[November 15]], [[2005]], artisans were removed as a playable [[W:c:swg:Professions (pre-NGE)|profession]],<ref name="NGE">{{SonyCite|url=players/content.vm?id=64886&resource=publish|text=Publish Archive 2005}}</ref> however the vehicle could still be [[W:c:swg:Crafting|crafted]] by players of the newly-created [[W:c:swg:Trader (Engineering)|engineering trader]] [[W:c:swg:Profession|profession]].<ref name="SchematicNGE" /> Capitalization of the speeder's name is somewhat inconsistent between sources. Two ''Star Wars Galaxies'' website articles presented the name as "AV-21 landspeeder,"<ref name="ConsumerReport1" /><ref name="ConsumerReport2">{{SonyCite|url=en_US/players/content.vm?id=66857&resource=features|text=''Corellia Times'' Consumer Reports: Vehicles}}</ref> as did the in-game description for the AV-21 schematic.<ref name="SchematicNGE" /> However, the majority of sources present the name fully capitalized as "AV-21 Landspeeder," including the description for the actual in-game speeder itself,<ref name="AV21Item">{{SWGcite|exp=AED|type=item|AV-21 Landspeeder}}</ref> as well as the description for the AV-21 power plant.<ref name="PowerPlant">{{SWGcite|exp=AED|type=item|Power Plant AV-21}}</ref>
